description: Close all 70 gaps identified in DEPLOYMENT-ANALYSIS.md: security hardening, legal compliance, broken feature fixes, distribution pipeline, backend hardening, website launch blockers, test coverage, and accessibility.

userStories.title: Bind MCP server to 127.0.0.1 and add token authentication
userStories.description: As a user, I want the MCP server to only listen on localhost and require a token so that remote and unauthorized processes cannot access my tools.
acceptanceCriteria: In MCPServerHost.start(), set NWParameters.requiredLocalEndpoint to NWEndpoint.hostPort(host: .ipv4(.loopback), port: ...) so the listener binds to 127.0.0.1 only
Add a static let authToken = UUID().uuidString property on MCPServerHost that is generated once at process start
Log the token at startup via GRumpLogger so the user can copy it
In handleRequest(), before processing any tools/call request, check for an 'auth_token' field in the JSON-RPC params; return JSON-RPC error -32600 if missing or wrong
tools/list remains accessible without auth (so clients can discover available tools)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Remove dangerous tools from MCP server and add path traversal protection
userStories.description: As a user, I want the MCP server to not expose get_env or run_command and to restrict file tools to the workspace root.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove 'run_command' from builtInToolDefs entirely (not just reject at dispatch time)
Remove 'get_env' from builtInToolDefs entirely

In the file-based tool handlers (read_file, write_file, edit_file, list_directory, search_files, grep_search), resolve the path and check it starts with the workspace root using resolvePath() and URL.standardized
If a path escapes the workspace root (e.g. ../../etc/passwd), return an error message 'Path is outside workspace root'
Add a workspaceRoot property to MCPServerHost that defaults to FileManager.default.currentDirectoryPath
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Route run_command through exec approval pipeline
userStories.description: As a user, I want run_command to go through the same security approval workflow as system_run so my configured security level is respected.
acceptanceCriteria: In ChatViewModel+ToolExecution.swift, change the run_command case to call executeSystemRun(args) instead of calling runShellCommand() directly
The args dictionary for run_command uses key 'command' while system_run uses key 'command' — verify they match or remap
If the user's exec approval config denies the command, run_command returns the denial message (same as system_run)
If the user's exec approval config allows the command, run_command executes normally
Existing system_run behavior is unchanged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Convert shell helpers to Process() argument arrays
userStories.description: As a user, I want shell tool helpers to use Process() with argument arrays instead of string interpolation to prevent command injection.
acceptanceCriteria: executeListProcesses: Use Process() with launchPath /bin/ps and arguments ['-eo', 'pid,comm'], then filter results in Swift code using String.contains instead of piping through grep
executeWhich: Use Process() with launchPath /usr/bin/which and arguments [name] — validate name contains only alphanumeric, dash, underscore, period characters first
executeDiskUsage: Use Process() with launchPath /bin/df and arguments ['-h', path] instead of shell string

executeSpotlightSearch in ToolExec+AppleNative.swift: Use Process() with launchPath /usr/bin/mdfind and arguments array instead of converting back to shell string; limit results in Swift code instead of piping through head
All four functions produce the same output format as before
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix backend credit deduction race condition with atomic SQL
userStories.description: As a user, I want credit deduction to be atomic so concurrent requests cannot over-spend my balance.
acceptanceCriteria: In backend/proxy.js, replace the read-compute-write credit deduction with a single atomic SQL UPDATE: UPDATE users SET credits_balance = MAX(0, credits_balance - ?) WHERE id = ? AND credits_balance >= ?
Check the number of rows affected — if 0 rows updated, the user has insufficient credits
Remove the separate SELECT that reads the current balance before deducting
Add a pre-request credit balance check: before proxying to OpenRouter, query the user's credits_balance and reject with 402 if balance <= 0
cd backend && npm test passes (existing tests still pass)

userStories.title: Fix DB initialization ordering and add global Express error handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the database to be initialized before any route runs and unhandled errors to return clean JSON responses.
acceptanceCriteria: In backend/server.js, call await initDatabase() at module load time (top-level await) OR move ensureDb() middleware registration to before all route definitions
Add a global Express error handler at the bottom of server.js after all routes: app.use((err, req, res, next) => { console.error(err); res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal server error' }); })
The error handler must NOT leak stack traces — only log them server-side
Health check endpoint at /api/health no longer exposes whether Stripe/OpenRouter keys are configured — only report 'configured: true/false' without key details
cd backend && npm test passes

userStories.title: Enforce wss:// for non-localhost OpenClaw URLs and validate scheme
userStories.description: As a user, I want OpenClaw connections to non-localhost servers to require encrypted WebSocket (wss://) so my data is not sent in plaintext.
acceptanceCriteria: In OpenClawService.swift connect(), after parsing the URL, check url.scheme
If url.host is not '127.0.0.1' and not 'localhost' and not '::1', require url.scheme == 'wss'
If a remote URL uses 'ws://' scheme, set connectionState = .error('Remote connections require wss:// (encrypted)')' and return without connecting
Local URLs (127.0.0.1, localhost, ::1) are allowed with ws:// or wss://
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Security issue from Section 3 of DEPLOYMENT-ANALYSIS.md

userStories.title: Remove fabricated aggregate rating from website JSON-LD
userStories.description: As a product owner, I want the website's structured data to not contain fabricated review counts that violate Google's guidelines.
acceptanceCriteria: In RE/website/src/app/layout.tsx, remove the entire aggregateRating object from the JSON-LD schema (ratingValue: '4.9', ratingCount: '2400')
Keep the rest of the JSON-LD Organization schema intact
No TypeScript errors after the change

userStories.title: Complete privacy manifest with missing required entries
userStories.description: As an app developer, I want the privacy manifest to declare all required-reason APIs so the app is not rejected by Apple.
acceptanceCriteria: In Sources/GRump/Resources/PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy, add NSPasteboard access declaration under NSPrivacyAccessedAPITypes with reason code 'CA92.1' (user-initiated clipboard paste)
Add NSPrivacyCollectedDataType entry for 'NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeUserContent' with purpose 'NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypePurposeAppFunctionality' and linked to user = true
Add NSPrivacyCollectedDataType entry for 'NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeOtherDiagnosticData' covering active app name tracking
The plist remains valid XML
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Privacy blocker from Section 3 of DEPLOYMENT-ANALYSIS.md

userStories.title: Flip ambient monitoring defaults to opt-in
userStories.description: As a privacy-conscious user, I want ambient monitoring features to be opt-in by default so my activity is not tracked without consent.
acceptanceCriteria: In AmbientMonitor.swift, change appTrackingEnabled default from true to false
Change windowTitleTrackingEnabled default from true to false
Change clipboardMonitorEnabled default (verify it's already false, keep it false)
The onboarding flow or settings should make it clear these can be enabled
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement WritingToolsService with real AI calls
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Writing Tools feature to produce real AI-generated text instead of hardcoded placeholder strings.
acceptanceCriteria: In WritingToolsService.swift, replace the hardcoded '[AI suggestion based on: ...]' return in generateText() with a real call to the AI service
Use MultiProviderAIService or OpenRouterService to send the prompt and context as a chat completion request
Use the user's configured model (from ModelRouter or AppStorage)
If no API key is configured, return a clear error message: 'Please configure an API key in Settings to use Writing Tools'
If the AI call fails, return 'Writing Tools error: [error description]' instead of silently returning placeholder

Functions that call generateText() (generateCommitMessage, generateDocumentation, generateComments, improveText, generateReleaseNotes, generateAPIDocumentation) continue to work as before since they delegate to generateText()
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: App quality blocker A1 from DEPLOYMENT-ANALYSIS.md

userStories.title: Wire code font setting to CodeBlockView and MarkdownTextView
userStories.description: As a user, I want my chosen code font (from Settings) to be applied in code blocks and markdown code spans.
acceptanceCriteria: Read @AppStorage('CodeFont') in DesignTokens.swift or a new computed property that returns the user's chosen font name
In Typography code font properties (codeLarge, code, codeSmall, codeMicro), use the stored font name if set, falling back to .monospaced
CodeBlockView.swift reads the code font setting and applies it to its text content
MarkdownTextView.swift applies the code font setting to inline code spans
If the chosen font name (e.g. 'Fira Code', 'JetBrains Mono') is not installed, fall back gracefully to system monospace
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: App quality blocker A2 from DEPLOYMENT-ANALYSIS.md

userStories.title: Wire line spacing setting to MarkdownTextView
userStories.description: As a user, I want my chosen line spacing (from Settings) to be applied in message text instead of using hardcoded values.
acceptanceCriteria: In MarkdownTextView.swift, replace hardcoded .lineSpacing(3) and .lineSpacing(2) with a value derived from @AppStorage('LineSpacing')
Define a reasonable range for line spacing (e.g. 0.0 to 10.0) with default of 3.0 for paragraphs and 2.0 for list items
The stored value acts as a multiplier or direct value for paragraph line spacing
List items use a proportionally smaller spacing (e.g. stored value * 0.67)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: App quality blocker A3 from DEPLOYMENT-ANALYSIS.md

userStories.title: Fix git shortcuts popover commit command and pipe reading
userStories.description: As a user, I want git shortcuts to work correctly — commit should include a message flag and output should be captured.
acceptanceCriteria: In StatusBarView.swift GitShortcutsPopover, change the Commit shortcut from runGitCommand('commit') to prompt the user for a commit message first or use runGitCommand('commit -m "<message>"')
Alternative: change Commit to run 'commit --allow-empty-message -m ""' as a quick commit, or show a text field for the message
In runGitCommand(), read standardOutput and standardError pipes after process terminates
Display the git command output in a result area or log it via GRumpLogger
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: App quality blocker A5 from DEPLOYMENT-ANALYSIS.md

userStories.title: Fix notification approval handler to use specific approvalId
userStories.description: As a user, I want notification-based approvals to resolve the correct pending approval instead of a random one.
acceptanceCriteria: In NotificationService.swift, set pendingApprovalId when a new approval notification is sent (in notifyApprovalNeeded or equivalent)
In the notification response handler (UNUserNotificationCenterDelegate), extract the approvalId from the notification's userInfo dictionary
Use the extracted approvalId to resolve the specific pending approval, not whatever happens to be current
Clear pendingApprovalId after the approval is resolved
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: App quality issue from Section 5 of DEPLOYMENT-ANALYSIS.md

userStories.title: Start ConnectionMonitor at app lifecycle and gate streaming
userStories.description: As a user, I want the connection monitor to start when the app launches and streaming to be blocked when offline.
acceptanceCriteria: In GRumpApp.swift, call ConnectionMonitor.shared.start() during app initialization (alongside other service starts like globalHotkey.start(), proactiveEngine.bootstrap())
Do NOT start it only from ChatTopBarView.onAppear — it must start regardless of UI state
In ChatViewModel+Streaming.swift, before calling the streaming API, check ConnectionMonitor.shared.canStream
If canStream is false, set an error message like 'You appear to be offline. Check your internet connection and try again.' instead of starting the stream
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix WritingTools availability check for correct OS versions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the WritingTools availability check to require macOS 15+ / iOS 18+ since that's when Apple's Writing Tools framework was introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: In WritingToolsService.swift, change checkWritingToolsAvailability() to return true only on macOS 15+ or iOS 18+, not macOS 14+
Use #available(macOS 15, iOS 18, *) instead of the current macOS 14 check
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: App quality issue from Section 5 of DEPLOYMENT-ANALYSIS.md

userStories.title: Replace Google OAuth placeholder in Info.plist
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Info.plist to not contain a literal placeholder string that gets registered as a URL scheme.
acceptanceCriteria: In Sources/GRump/Info.plist, find CFBundleURLSchemes containing 'com.googleusercontent.apps.YOUR_CLIENT_ID'
Remove the entire CFBundleURLTypes entry that contains this placeholder
Add a comment in the plist or a note in CLAUDE.md that Google OAuth URL scheme should be added when a real client ID is obtained
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Synchronize version numbers across Info.plist, project.yml, and create-release.sh
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all version references to agree so builds and releases are consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: Set version to 2.0.0 in Info.plist (CFBundleShortVersionString) — keep existing value
Update project.yml MARKETING_VERSION from 1.0.0 to 2.0.0
Update scripts/create-release.sh VERSION variable from v1.0.0 to v2.0.0
All three files now report the same version: 2.0.0
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix Settings Check for Updates toggle to use Sparkle
userStories.description: As a user, I want the 'Check for updates on launch' toggle to actually trigger Sparkle's update check instead of opening a browser.
acceptanceCriteria: In Settings+TabViews.swift (or equivalent settings file), find the 'Check for updates' action
Replace the current NSWorkspace.shared.open(URL(string: 'https://www.g-rump.com/releases')!) call with a call to SparkleUpdateService.shared.checkForUpdates() (or equivalent)
If SparkleUpdateService is not configured (no SUFeedURL), show an alert saying 'Auto-update is not yet configured'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Parameterize create-release.sh for reuse
userStories.description: As a developer, I want create-release.sh to accept version and repo as parameters instead of hardcoded values.
acceptanceCriteria: Accept VERSION as first argument ($1) with fallback to current hardcoded value
Accept REPO as second argument ($2) with fallback to current hardcoded value
Add usage message if --help is passed
Update the REPO variable from 'Aphrodine-wq/G-Rump_MAC_OS' to match the actual repository
Script still works when called without arguments (uses defaults)

userStories.title: Handle invoice.payment_succeeded Stripe webhook
userStories.description: As a paying user, I want my subscription to remain active after renewal so my tier and credits update correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: In backend/stripe.js webhook handler, add a case for 'invoice.payment_succeeded'
Extract customer ID from the invoice event and look up the user
Update subscription_status to 'active' and refresh subscription_period_end from the invoice's period_end
If the invoice has a subscription, update the user's tier based on the subscription's price ID
cd backend && npm test passes

userStories.title: Handle customer.subscription.paused Stripe webhook
userStories.description: As a product owner, I want paused subscriptions to correctly downgrade the user's tier.
acceptanceCriteria: In backend/stripe.js webhook handler, add a case for 'customer.subscription.paused'
Extract customer ID and look up user
Set subscription_status to 'paused' and tier to 'free'
Also handle 'customer.subscription.past_due' the same way (set status to 'past_due')
cd backend && npm test passes
userStories.notes: Backend item from Section 4 of DEPLOYMENT-ANALYSIS.md

userStories.title: Validate Stripe price IDs on startup and remove invalid fallbacks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the backend to fail fast if Stripe price IDs are missing rather than silently using invalid placeholder IDs.
acceptanceCriteria: In backend/stripe.js, remove the fallback strings like 'price_starter_monthly' from the PRICE_IDS object
Add a validateStripeConfig() function that checks if all required price env vars are set when STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is present
If STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is set but any STRIPE_PRICE_* env var is missing, throw an error at startup with a clear message listing which vars are missing
If STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is not set, skip validation (billing disabled mode)
cd backend && npm test passes

userStories.title: Validate avatarUrl and add compression middleware
userStories.description: As a developer, I want user-submitted avatar URLs to be validated and API responses to be compressed.
acceptanceCriteria: In backend/server.js PATCH /api/me route, validate avatarUrl with new URL(avatarUrl) wrapped in try/catch
Reject avatarUrl if scheme is not 'https' — return 400 with message 'Avatar URL must use HTTPS'
Reject avatarUrl if the URL constructor throws — return 400 with message 'Invalid avatar URL'
Add compression middleware: npm install compression, then app.use(compression()) after helmet middleware
cd backend && npm test passes

userStories.title: Add migration version table, enable foreign keys, and add missing indexes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want database migrations to be tracked, foreign keys enforced, and queries indexed.
acceptanceCriteria: In backend/db.js initDatabase(), add PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON after client creation
Create a migrations table: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS migrations (version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')))
Wrap existing migration functions in version checks (e.g. version 1 = initial schema, version 2 = profile columns, version 3 = stripe columns)
Add CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_usage_log_created ON usage_log(created_at)
Add CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_usage_log_user_created ON usage_log(user_id, created_at)
cd backend && npm test passes

userStories.title: Add Vercel cron for log pruning and increase maxDuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want old usage logs to be pruned on a schedule and streaming requests to have enough time to complete.
acceptanceCriteria: In backend/vercel.json, increase maxDuration from 60 to 300
Add a crons configuration in vercel.json: { 'crons': [{ 'path': '/api/cron/prune', 'schedule': '0 3 * * *' }] }
Create backend/api/cron/prune.js (or add a /api/cron/prune route to server.js) that calls pruneOldUsageLogs()
The cron endpoint should verify it's called by Vercel's cron system (check CRON_SECRET header or similar)
Remove the setInterval-based prune logic from server.js since it never runs on Vercel
cd backend && npm test passes

userStories.title: Replace model tier string-includes with maintained allowlist
userStories.description: As a developer, I want model tier classification to use an explicit allowlist so new models don't silently fall to the wrong pricing tier.
acceptanceCriteria: In backend/proxy.js, replace the model.includes(':free') and model.includes('claude-opus') pattern with a MODEL_TIERS object
Define MODEL_TIERS with explicit model name patterns: { frontier: ['claude-opus', 'claude-sonnet', 'gpt-4o', 'kimi-k2'], free: [':free'], fast: [] } where fast is the default
Create a getModelTier(modelName) function that checks against the allowlist
Use the tier-based credit multiplier from db.js CREDITS_PER_1K_BY_MODEL_TIER instead of flat 1 credit per 1K tokens
Log a warning when an unknown model falls to default tier so it can be classified later
cd backend && npm test passes

userStories.title: Add Stripe idempotency keys to API calls
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Stripe API calls to use idempotency keys so network retries don't create duplicate charges.
acceptanceCriteria: In backend/stripe.js, add idempotencyKey to stripe.checkout.sessions.create() calls — use a hash of userId + priceId + timestamp
Add idempotencyKey to stripe.customers.create() calls — use a hash of userId + email
Import crypto module and create a helper: generateIdempotencyKey(...parts) that returns crypto.createHash('sha256').update(parts.join(':')).digest('hex')
cd backend && npm test passes

userStories.title: Add structured JSON logging with request IDs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want backend logs to be structured JSON with request IDs for easier debugging and log aggregation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a logger utility in backend/logger.js that wraps console.log/error/warn with JSON output: { timestamp, level, requestId, message, ...extra }
Add request ID middleware early in server.js: generate a UUID per request and attach it to req.requestId
Replace key console.log calls in proxy.js, auth.js, and stripe.js with the structured logger
Include requestId in error responses so users can reference it in support requests
cd backend && npm test passes

userStories.title: Add JWT refresh endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want my session to be extended without re-authenticating with Google every 7 days.
acceptanceCriteria: In backend/auth.js, add a refreshToken(oldToken) function that verifies the old token, checks it's not expired by more than 24 hours, and issues a new token
In backend/server.js, add POST /api/auth/refresh route that accepts { token } in body and returns { token: newToken }
The refresh route requires the old token to be valid (or recently expired within 24h grace period)
New token has the same 7-day expiry starting from now
Rate limit the refresh endpoint to prevent abuse (10 per hour per user)
cd backend && npm test passes

userStories.title: Create Privacy Policy page for website
userStories.description: As a user, I want to read the privacy policy before using the product, and as a product owner I need it for legal compliance.
acceptanceCriteria: Create RE/website/src/app/privacy/page.tsx with a comprehensive privacy policy page

Cover: data collected (chat messages sent to AI providers, usage analytics, account info), data storage (Turso DB, Keychain for API keys), third-party services (OpenRouter, Anthropic, OpenAI, Stripe, Google Sign-In), data retention (90-day usage logs), user rights (export via /api/me/export, deletion via DELETE /api/me), cookies, children's privacy (no users under 13)
Include the company name and contact email from existing legal references
Update the footer Privacy Policy link from href='#' to href='/privacy'
Page renders without TypeScript errors

userStories.title: Create Terms of Service page for website
userStories.description: As a product owner, I need terms of service for legal compliance with Stripe, Apple, and Google requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: Create RE/website/src/app/terms/page.tsx with terms of service

Cover: acceptable use (no abuse of AI tools, no illegal activities), payment terms (subscription billing, credit purchases, refund policy), intellectual property (user owns their content, AI output disclaimer), limitation of liability, termination, governing law
Reference the privacy policy page
Update the footer Terms of Service link from href='#' to href='/terms'
Page renders without TypeScript errors

userStories.title: Fix website social links and download button hrefs
userStories.description: As a website visitor, I want social media links and download buttons to point to real URLs instead of href='#'.
acceptanceCriteria: In the website footer component, replace Twitter/X href='#' with a placeholder URL like 'https://twitter.com/grumpapp' (or remove the link if no account exists and add a comment)
Replace Discord href='#' with a placeholder or remove
Replace GitHub href='#' with the actual repository URL if public, or remove
For 'Download for Mac' CTAs, set href to '/releases' or a direct download URL placeholder with a TODO comment
No href='#' links remain in the footer or hero sections
No TypeScript errors
userStories.notes: Website items from Section 7 of DEPLOYMENT-ANALYSIS.md

userStories.title: Add better-sqlite3 to devDependencies for backend tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want backend tests to work on a clean npm install by having all test dependencies declared.
acceptanceCriteria: Run cd backend && npm install --save-dev better-sqlite3
Verify better-sqlite3 appears in backend/package.json devDependencies
cd backend && npm test passes on a clean install

userStories.title: Add Stripe webhook integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want test coverage for Stripe webhook handlers so billing regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a describe('Stripe Webhooks') section to backend/tests/backend.test.js (or create backend/tests/stripe.test.js)
Test checkout.session.completed: mock event with customer, subscription, metadata.userId — verify user tier is updated
Test customer.subscription.deleted: verify user tier reverts to 'free'
Test invoice.payment_failed: verify subscription_status is updated
Test invoice.payment_succeeded (the new handler from US-022): verify subscription_status and period_end are updated
All tests use in-memory SQLite (better-sqlite3), no real Stripe API calls

userStories.title: Add credit deduction race condition test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify that concurrent credit deductions cannot over-spend a user's balance.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a describe('Credit Deduction') section to backend/tests/backend.test.js
Test: create user with 10 credits, call deductCredits(userId, 10) twice concurrently using Promise.all, verify final balance is 0 (not negative)
Test: create user with 5 credits, attempt to deduct 10, verify balance is 0 and the function returns failure or partial deduction
Test: create user with 0 credits, attempt to deduct 1, verify rejection
cd backend && npm test passes
userStories.notes: Test item 57 from DEPLOYMENT-ANALYSIS.md. Depends on US-005

userStories.title: Add backend auth middleware HTTP tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify that protected routes reject unauthenticated and expired token requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a describe('Auth Middleware') section to backend/tests/backend.test.js
Test: GET /api/me without Authorization header returns 401
Test: GET /api/me with invalid token returns 401
Test: GET /api/me with expired token returns 401
Test: GET /api/me with valid token returns 200 with user data
Use supertest or direct HTTP requests against the Express app
cd backend && npm test passes

userStories.title: Add backend API route integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests covering the main API routes for reliability.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a describe('API Routes') section to backend/tests/backend.test.js
Test: GET /api/health returns 200 with status and uptime
Test: POST /api/auth/google with invalid token returns 401
Test: PATCH /api/me with invalid avatarUrl returns 400
Test: GET /api/me/export returns user data JSON for authenticated user

userStories.title: Fix SpotlightIndexer to use async API off main thread
userStories.description: As a user, I want Spotlight indexing to not block the main thread.
acceptanceCriteria: In SpotlightIndexer.swift indexConversation(), wrap the CSSearchableIndex call in Task.detached or move to a background queue
Use the async version of CSSearchableIndex.indexSearchableItems() if available on macOS 14+, otherwise dispatch to a background queue
Verify the function is not called on @MainActor (remove @MainActor if present, or use nonisolated)

userStories.title: Add accessibilityLabel to StatusBarView connection dot
userStories.description: As a VoiceOver user, I want the connection status indicator to be announced properly.
acceptanceCriteria: In StatusBarView.swift, find the connection status dot (Circle or similar shape)
Add .accessibilityLabel() that describes the current state: 'Connected', 'Disconnected', or 'Connection degraded'
Fix the connection indicator to use ConnectionMonitor.shared.status instead of just checking platformUser != nil
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Accessibility items 68 + quality fix from DEPLOYMENT-ANALYSIS.md

userStories.title: Fix StreamErrorView to respect reduceMotion
userStories.description: As a user with motion sensitivity, I want error animations to be reduced when I have reduceMotion enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: In StreamErrorView.swift (or wherever the stream error card is defined), add @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) var reduceMotion
When reduceMotion is true, replace spring/bounce animations with simple .opacity transitions
When reduceMotion is true, skip any shake or slide animations
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add VoiceOver announcements for key state changes
userStories.description: As a VoiceOver user, I want to hear announcements when important state changes occur.
acceptanceCriteria: When SplashScreenView completes and transitions to main content, post an AccessibilityNotification.Announcement with 'G-Rump is ready'
When StreamErrorView appears, post an announcement with the error message text
When onboarding advances to the next step, post an announcement with the step title
Use AccessibilityNotification.Announcement (macOS 14+) or UIAccessibility.post(notification:argument:) equivalent
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add consent prompt at onboarding before first AI call
userStories.description: As a privacy-conscious user, I want to consent to my data being sent to AI providers before my first message is processed.
acceptanceCriteria: In OnboardingView.swift, add a consent step (can be part of an existing step or a new step) that explains: 'Your messages will be sent to AI providers (OpenRouter, Anthropic, OpenAI) for processing. Your data is not used for training.'
Include a link to the privacy policy page
Add a 'I agree' button or checkbox that must be checked before proceeding
Store consent in @AppStorage('PrivacyConsentGiven') with default false
If consent is not given, do not allow sending messages (show the consent view instead)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix CI print() scan to be recursive and make SwiftLint blocking
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CI to catch print() statements in subdirectories and SwiftLint failures to block merges.
acceptanceCriteria: In .github/workflows/ci.yml lint job, change the print() check from grep on Sources/GRump/*.swift to grep -r on Sources/GRump/ to cover subdirectories like Views/
Remove || true from the SwiftLint command so lint failures cause the job to fail
Keep the outer if command -v swiftlint check so CI doesn't fail if SwiftLint is not installed
Force-unwrap check should exit 1 if violations found (currently informational only) — make it a warning step, not blocking
userStories.notes: CI item from Section 8 of DEPLOYMENT-ANALYSIS.md

userStories.title: Reconcile domain references across codebase
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all domain references to be consistent — using g-rump.com everywhere.
acceptanceCriteria: Search for 'grump.app' across the entire codebase (excluding node_modules)
Replace 'grump.app' with 'g-rump.com' in LICENSE file
Replace 'legal@grump.app' with 'legal@g-rump.com' in LICENSE file
Replace any other 'grump.app' references with 'g-rump.com' (check CLAUDE.md, README.md, etc.)
Do NOT change bundle identifier 'com.grump.app' — that is different from a domain name
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add CI release artifact upload step
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CI release builds to upload artifacts so they are not immediately discarded.
acceptanceCriteria: In .github/workflows/ci.yml release job, add an actions/upload-artifact@v4 step after the build step
Upload the .app bundle from the build output directory
Set artifact name to 'G-Rump-${{ github.sha }}' and retention-days to 30
The release job should build a universal binary (arm64 + x86_64) matching what package.sh produces

userStories.title: Dynamic Type support in Typography tokens
userStories.description: As a user who needs larger text, I want G-Rump to respect my system text size setting.
acceptanceCriteria: In DesignTokens.swift, refactor Typography static properties to use Font.system(.body), Font.system(.headline), Font.system(.caption) etc. from the system type scale instead of Font.system(size: N) with hardcoded pixel sizes
For code fonts, use @ScaledMetric to scale the base size relative to the user's text size setting
Keep the existing size relationships (heading1 > heading2 > heading3 > body > caption etc.) but use relative sizing
Add a Typography.scaled(baseSize:relativeTo:) helper if needed for custom scaling
Existing UI layouts should not break at larger text sizes (may need some .minimumScaleFactor or .lineLimit adjustments in tight spaces)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Accessibility item 65 from DEPLOYMENT-ANALYSIS.md — refactored to use system text styles (.body, .headline, .caption, etc.)
